title: Chapter 1 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the basics: puts, print, gets, comments, and objects!

questions.question: Who created the Ruby programming language?
questions.explanation: Yukihiro 'Matz' Matsumoto created Ruby in 1995 to be a natural, human-friendly programming language.

questions.question: Which framework made Ruby famously popular for building full-stack web applications?
questions.explanation: Ruby on Rails built some of the biggest websites in the world, like GitHub and Shopify.

questions.question: If you want to display text to the screen AND automatically ensure a new line is attached at the end, what command do you use?
questions.explanation: `puts` (put string) automatically appends a new line sequence to the given output.

questions.question: If you use the `print` command sequentially, what happens?
options: Each output is on a new line.
The subsequent output is mushed together on the same line.
A space is automatically added between them.
questions.explanation: `print` does NOT append a new line, so consecutive `print` statements print right next to each other.

questions.question: What does the `gets` method do?
options: It downloads a file from the internet.
It waits for the user to type something and hit Enter.
It grabs a variable from a different file.
It generates a random string of letters.
questions.explanation: `gets` means 'get string'. It pauses the program to capture keyboard input.

questions.question: When you capture user input using `gets`, what happens if you don't use `.chomp`?
options: The string is saved in all lowercase.
The string contains an extra invisible 'Enter' (new line) character at the end.
questions.explanation: When you hit Enter to submit your text, Ruby includes that Enter stroke (`\n`). Using `.chomp` removes it.

questions.question: To strip the trailing new line from an input string, attach ._____ to the end of gets.
questions.explanation: You "chomp" off the extra Enter character.

questions.question: Which symbol is used to create a single-line comment in Ruby?
questions.explanation: The hash/pound symbol `#` makes everything after it on the same line a comment.

questions.question: Comments are executed by the Ruby interpreter just like normal code.
questions.explanation: Comments are completely ignored by the computer. They are strictly notes for humans.

questions.question: What is the core philosophical rule of the Ruby language?
questions.explanation: Ruby treats absolutely every piece of data, including numbers and text, as objects with built-in abilities.

questions.question: Which symbol is used for "dot notation" to call a method on an object?
questions.explanation: The dot `.` is used to invoke a method, for example: `"hello".upcase`.

questions.question: Because everything in Ruby is an object, a number like `5` has its own built-in methods.
questions.explanation: Yes! You can call methods directly on numbers, e.g., `5.even?`

questions.question: What would `puts "hello".upcase` output?
questions.explanation: The `.upcase` method converts the string to uppercase letters.

questions.question: What would `puts "dog".length` output?
questions.explanation: The word "dog" has 3 letters, so its length is 3.

questions.question: What is MINSWAN in the Ruby community?
options: Matz is nice so we are nice.
questions.explanation: MINSWAN means 'Matz is nice so we are nice', summarizing the friendly nature of the Ruby community.

questions.question: Can you have a comment on the same line as running code?
options: Yes, as long as it comes after the code.
Yes, but only if it comes before the code.
No, comments must be on their own isolated lines.
questions.explanation: Yes, this is an 'inline' comment. Any code written before the `#` sign will run normally.

questions.question: The primary goal of Ruby was to make computers run as fast as possible.
questions.explanation: The primary goal was developer happiness and writing elegant, simple code.

questions.question: What is the result of `puts "ruby".reverse`?
questions.explanation: The `.reverse` method simply reverses the string to "ybur".

questions.question: To display text and stop the cursor on the exact same line, you should use the _____ command.
questions.explanation: `print` displays output exactly without modifying new lines.

questions.question: The invisible line break character is created anytime a user hits the _____ key.
questions.explanation: Hitting Enter generates a new line, which gets grabbed by the `gets` method unless `.chomp` is used.
